program_name: USDA - Rural Energy for America Program (REAP) Grants
items: Geothermal Electric, Solar Photovoltaics, Wind (All), Biomass, Hydrogen
technologies: Geothermal Electric, Solar Photovoltaics, Wind (All), Biomass, Hydrogen, Solar Water Heat, Solar Space Heat, Solar Thermal Electric, Hydroelectric, Geothermal Heat Pumps, Combined Heat & Power, Tidal, Wave, Ocean Thermal, Wind (Small), Hydroelectric (Small), Geothermal Direct-Use, Anaerobic Digestion, Fuel Cells using Renewable Fuels, Microturbines
incentive_description: Note: The U.S. Department of Agriculture's Rural Development issues periodic Notices of Solicitation of Applications for the Rural Energy for America Program (REAP) in the Federal Register. The Rural Energy for America Program (REAP) provides financial assistance to agricultural producers and rural small businesses in America to purchase, install, and construct renewable energy systems, make energy efficiency improvements to non-residential buildings and facilities, use renewable technologies that reduce energy consumption, and participate in energy audits and renewable energy development assistance. Grants are limited to 25% of a proposed project's cost. Loan guarantees may not exceed $25 million. The combined amount of a grant and loan guarantee must be at least $5,000 (with the grant portion at least $1,500) and may not exceed 75% of the project's cost. Application due dates are published annually in the Notice of Funding Availability.
incentive_narrative: Grant and loan guarantee financing available; combined grant+guarantee must be at least $5,000 (grant at least $1,500) and may not exceed 75% of project cost. Loan guarantee cap typically $25,000,000; program dedicates a portion of funds to small grants ($20,000 or less).
application_process: Contact the program administrator or visit the program website to apply. Review eligibility requirements before starting your project. Application due dates are published annually in the Notice of Funding Availability.
notes: Renewable Grants: up to $1 million. Efficiency Grants: up to $500,000. Loan and Grant Combination: Grant portion must exceed $1,500. Maximum: 25% of cost for most projects. Grants and guaranteed loans generally available to small businesses and agricultural producers; applicants must demonstrate sufficient revenue to cover operations, maintenance, and any debt service. Last updated: 2024-08-28.

program_name: Renewable Electricity Production Tax Credit (PTC)
items: Geothermal Electric, Solar Thermal Electric, Solar Photovoltaics, Wind (All), Wind (Small), Offshore Wind, Hydroelectric, Municipal Solid Waste, Landfill Gas, Tidal, Wave, Ocean Thermal, Hydroelectric (Small)
technologies: Geothermal Electric, Solar Thermal Electric, Solar Photovoltaics, Wind (All), Wind (Small), Offshore Wind, Hydroelectric, Municipal Solid Waste, Landfill Gas, Tidal, Wave, Ocean Thermal, Hydroelectric (Small)
incentive_description: The federal renewable electricity production tax credit (PTC) is an inflation-adjusted per-kilowatt-hour (kWh) tax credit for electricity generated by qualified energy resources and sold by the taxpayer to an unrelated person during the taxable year. The duration of the credit is 10 years after the date the facility is placed in service. Systems commencing construction on or after 2022 and meeting labor requirements, and systems under 1 MW: Wind, Closed-Loop Biomass, Solar, Geothermal: $0.0275/kWh. Other eligible technologies: $0.015/kWh. Systems over 1 MW commencing construction on or after 2022 and NOT meeting labor requirements: Wind, Closed-Loop Biomass, Solar, Geothermal: $0.0055/kWh. Other eligible technologies: $0.003/kWh. Applies to first 10 years of operation. Note: The One Big Beautiful Bill made significant changes; solar and wind must be placed in service by 2027-12-31 or construction commence by 2026-07-04. Executive Order (2025-07-07) and related guidance affect eligibility and foreign assistance restrictions.
special_requirements: None specified
application_process: Contact the program administrator or visit the program website to apply. Review eligibility requirements before starting your project.
notes: Systems commencing construction on or after 2022 and meeting labor requirements, and systems under 1 MW: Wind, Closed-Loop Biomass, Solar, Geothermal: $0.0275/kWh; Other eligible technologies: $0.015/kWh. Systems over 1 MW not meeting labor requirements: Wind/Closed-Loop Biomass/Solar/Geothermal: $0.0055/kWh; Other: $0.003/kWh. Maximum Incentive: None specified. Carryover provisions: Unused credits may be carried forward for up to 20 years or carried back 1 year. Last updated: 2025-07-18.

program_name: Plug-In Electric Drive Vehicle Tax Credit
items: Passenger Electric Vehicles, Plug-in Electric Hybrid Vehicles
technologies: Passenger Electric Vehicles, Plug-in Electric Hybrid Vehicles
incentive_description: Note: Section 70502 of The One Big Beautiful Bill (OBBB) repealed this tax credit for equipment placed in service after September 30, 2025. Vehicles placed into service prior to January 1, 2023: The federal government provides a tax credit for the purchase of a new all electric or plug-in hybrid electric vehicle. The credit has a base value of $2,500 and an additional $417 for each kWh of battery capacity in excess of 4 kWh, up to a combined maximum of $7,500. Vehicles placed into service after December 31, 2022 and before September 30, 2025: Qualifying vehicles can receive up to $7,500 if certain component sourcing and final assembly requirements are met. MSRP and income limits apply. The Inflation Reduction Act established a process allowing the purchaser to transfer the credit to the dealer.
special_requirements: Vehicles must be made by a manufacturer, have GVWR ≤ 14,000 pounds, and have battery capacity ≥ 4 kWh. Final assembly in North America and component sourcing/recycling percentages apply for credits after 12/31/2022. MSRP and modified adjusted gross income limits apply.
application_process: Contact the program administrator or visit the program website to apply. Review eligibility requirements before starting your project.
notes: Vehicles purchased prior to 1/1/2023: $2,500 plus $417 for each kWh of battery capacity in excess of 4 kWh. Vehicles purchased after 12/31/2022: Up to $7,500 if certain sourcing requirements are met. Maximum incentive: $7,500. Last updated: 2025-07-25.

technologies: Level-2 Electric Vehicle Service Equipment, Direct Current Fast Charging Equipment
incentive_description: Note: Section 70504 of The One Big Beautiful Bill (OBBB) repealed this tax credit for equipment placed in service after June 30, 2026. Prior to repeal, qualified alternative fuel vehicle refueling equipment, including electric vehicle charging equipment, was eligible for a tax credit of 6% up to $100,000 for each single item of property. Projects that met specified prevailing wage and apprenticeship requirements were entitled to a larger tax credit of 30%. The direct pay option allowed certain non-taxable entities to monetize the credit. The credit could be transferred subject to IRS reporting and limitations.
recipient: Business, Industrial Facility, Local Government, Nonprofit Organization, Contractor
special_requirements: Project must be located in a census tract described in section 45D(e) of the IRS Code, or not in an urban area. Prevailing wage and apprenticeship requirements apply for the enhanced 30% credit; labor provisions effective for projects with construction beginning 60 days or more after Treasury guidance publication.
customer_type: Commercial, Industrial, Local Government, Nonprofit, Cooperative Utilities, State Government, Tribal Government
application_process: Corporate taxpayers claim the credit on their federal tax returns per IRS guidance. Non‑taxable entities may use the direct pay election; transfers and other elections must be reported to the IRS by the due date for the tax filing for the year the credit is claimed.
notes: 6% base credit; 30% credit for projects meeting specified labor standards. Maximum: $100,000 for each single item of property. Credit repealed for equipment placed in service after 2026-06-30. Last updated: 2025-07-25.

incentive_description: The Texas LoanSTAR (Saving Taxes and Resources) low-interest revolving loan program finances energy-related cost reduction retrofits for state, public school, college, university, and non-profit hospital facilities. Borrowers repay loans through the stream of cost savings realized from their energy cost-reduction projects. The LoanSTAR Program Administrator should be contacted for information on current loan interest rates. As of September 1, 2023, LoanSTAR has funded over 337 loans totaling over $600 million. Guidelines for project eligibility, fund availability and project funding and repayment are set forth in the administrative rules and the technical guidelines. Each April and October, the State Energy Conservation Office (SECO) publishes a Notice of Loan Fund Availability and request for applications of LoanSTAR loans. Applications are scored by a review committee; selected institutions will be asked to sign an MOU and submit required assessment reports. SECO performs design review and on-site construction monitoring. More information, including project applications and a detailed program guidebook, are available on the program website.
recipient: Local Government, Nonprofit Organization, Educational Institution, State Government
special_requirements: Guidelines for project eligibility, fund availability and project funding and repayment are set forth in the administrative rules and the technical guidelines. Each energy cost reduction measure must pay for itself within the estimated useful life of that measure.
customer_type: Local Government, Nonprofit, Schools, State Government
application_process: Contact the program administrator or visit the program website to apply. SECO issues Notices of Loan Fund Availability each April and October; applications are scored and highest scoring applicants receive funding commitments. Selected institutions sign an MOU and submit required assessment reports (EAR/UAR) within 120 days. Review the LoanSTAR technical guidelines and program materials on the website before applying.
notes: Loan term and interest rates are set with each Request for Application announcement. Loans are repaid through energy cost savings; most projects have a composite payback of 10 years or less. Authorities: 10 Tex. Gov. Code § 2305.032; 34 TAC § 19.41 et seq. Last updated: 2025-06-06.

program_name: Energy-Efficient Commercial Buildings Tax Deduction
items: Equipment Insulation, Water Heaters, Lighting, Lighting Controls/Sensors, Chillers, Furnaces, Boilers, Heat pumps, Air conditioners, Caulking/Weather-stripping, Duct/Air sealing, Building Insulation, Windows, Siding, Roofs, Comprehensive Measures/Whole Building, Tankless Water Heater
technologies: Equipment Insulation, Water Heaters, Lighting, Lighting Controls/Sensors, Chillers, Furnaces, Boilers, Heat pumps, Air conditioners, Caulking/Weather-stripping, Duct/Air sealing, Building Insulation, Windows, Siding, Roofs, Comprehensive Measures/Whole Building, Tankless Water Heater
incentive_description: Note: Section 70507 of The One Big Beautiful Bill (OBBB) repealed this tax credit for property the construction of which begins after June 30, 2026. The federal Energy Policy Act of 2005 established a tax deduction for energy-efficient commercial buildings applicable to qualifying systems and buildings placed in service from January 1, 2006. A tax deduction is available to owners of qualified commercial buildings and designers of energy efficient commercial building property (EECBP) or energy efficient commercial building retrofit property (EEBRP) installed in buildings owned by specified tax-exempt entities. EECBP must be installed on or in a building that is located in the U.S. and within the scope of a specified ASHRAE 90.1 and the Illuminating Engineering Society of North America. Property must be installed as part of interior lighting systems, heating, cooling, ventilation, and hot water systems, or the building envelope, and certified as part of a plan to reduce total annual energy and power costs by 25% or more compared to a reference building. Base deduction: $0.50 per square foot for a building with 25% energy savings, plus $0.02 per square foot for each percentage point above 25%, up to $1.00 per square foot for 50% savings. Bonus deduction (meets labor provisions): $2.50 per square foot for 25% savings, plus $0.10 per square foot per percentage point above 25%, up to $5.00 per square foot for 50% savings.
recipient: Business, Local Government, Nonprofit Organization, Educational Institution, Federal Government
special_requirements: Must meet ASHRAE 90.1 and Illuminating Engineering Society scope; property must be part of a plan to reduce total annual energy and power costs by 25% or more compared to a reference building. Projects meeting IRA/IRS Notice 2022-61 labor requirements may qualify for higher bonus deduction.
customer_type: Commercial, Construction, Nonprofit, Schools, State Government, Federal Government
application_process: Contact the program administrator or visit the program website to apply. Review eligibility requirements before starting your project.
notes: Base and bonus per-square-foot deduction tiers apply: Base $0.50/ft² (25% savings) + $0.02/ft² per point up to $1.00/ft² (50%). Bonus with labor provisions $2.50/ft² (25%) + $0.10/ft² per point up to $5.00/ft² (50%). Maximum incentive: project cost or $5.00 per square foot, whichever is less. Last updated: 2025-07-25.

incentive_description: Note: Applications are accepted year round. Financial investment decisions are made twice a year. See program website to learn more about the current status. USDA Rural Development is offering loan guarantees for the development, construction, and retrofitting of commercial-scale biorefineries. Eligible borrowers include individuals, entities, Indian tribes, state or local governments, corporations, farm cooperatives or farm cooperative organizations, associations of agricultural producers, National Laboratories, institutions of higher education, rural electric cooperatives, public power entities, and consortium of any of these types of entities. Financed entities must provide at least 20% of the financing for eligible project costs, and applications for funding must include an independent feasibility study and technical assessment. Eligible project costs include the purchase and installation of equipment, construction or retrofitting costs, permit and licensing fees, working capital, land acquisition, and the costs of financing. The project must be for the development and construction or retrofitting of a commercial-scale biorefinery using an eligible technology, must use an eligible feedstock for the production of advanced biofuels and biobased products, and the majority of the production must be advanced biofuels.

incentive_description: The Renewable Energy for America Program (REAP) Energy Audit and Renewable Energy Development Assistance Program (EA/REDA) provides assistance to agricultural producers and rural small businesses for energy audits and renewable energy technical assistance including renewable energy site assessments. Applicants must submit separate applications for assistance, limited to one energy audit and one REDA per fiscal year. The maximum aggregate amount of an energy audit and REDA grant in a Federal fiscal year is $100,000. Eligible project costs include salaries directly related to the project, travel expenses for conducting audits or assistance, office supplies, and administrative expenses up to 5% of the grant. Funds may not be used for construction-related activities, purchase or lease of equipment, payment of judgments or debt owed the government, costs to prepare an application, or political/lobbying activities. program_name: Residential Energy Conservation Subsidy Exclusion (Personal)
items: Solar Water Heat, Solar Space Heat, Solar Photovoltaics
technologies: Solar Water Heat, Solar Space Heat, Solar Photovoltaics
incentive_description: According to Section 136 of the U.S. Code, energy conservation subsidies provided (directly or indirectly) to customers by public utilities are non-taxable. This exclusion does not apply to electricity-generating systems registered as "qualifying facilities" under the Public Utility Regulatory Policies Act of 1978 (PURPA). If a taxpayer claims federal tax credits or deductions for the energy conservation property, the investment basis for the purpose of claiming the deduction or tax credit must be reduced by the value of the energy conservation subsidy. The term "energy conservation measure" includes installations or modifications primarily designed to reduce consumption of electricity or natural gas, or to improve the management of energy demand. Eligible dwelling units include houses, apartments, condominiums, mobile homes, boats and similar properties.
special_requirements: Must meet definitions of "energy conservation measure" per Section 136; subsidies for qualifying facilities (PURPA) are excluded
application_process: No application. Taxpayers should consult IRS guidance and a tax professional to determine applicability and basis adjustments when claiming credits or deductions.
notes: Incentive Amount: 100% of subsidy. Authority: 26 USC § 136 (enacted 1992; effective 1993). Last updated: 2025-05-16.

incentive_description: Through the Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP), the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) issues grants to states, territories, and some Indian tribes to improve the energy efficiency of low-income homes in their jurisdictions. States, territories and Indian governments contract with local governments and nonprofit agencies that provide the weatherization services. Low-income homes that qualify for the program will receive free weatherization services based on the needs of the home, and the rules established by the state. The average expenditure is $6,500. Applicants are automatically eligible if they receive Supplemental Security Income or Aid to Families with Dependent Children; states give preference to people over 60, families with a member with a disability, and families with children in most states.
special_requirements: Services must meet state rules; eligibility preferences include people over 60, families with a member with a disability, and families with children. Must meet state-defined requirements and federal guidelines.
application_process: Contact the state weatherization agency or visit the program website to apply. Review state-specific income eligibility and application procedures before starting the process.
notes: Free; specific improvements determined case-by-case depending on the needs of the home. Last updated: 2022-07-18.

program_name: Enroll Your Smart Thermostat to Claim an $85 Rebate
incentive_description:
Get an $85 rebate when you enroll your smart thermostat in the Con Edison Smart Usage Rewards program. This one-time payment is sent directly to you as soon as your enrollment is finalized, putting cash in your pocket while helping you lower your cooling costs.

To claim your $85, simply register an eligible smart thermostat and agree to brief, automatic temperature adjustments during periods of high electricity demand. These events typically happen on weekdays and last about four hours. You always maintain full control of your comfort and can override the settings at any time if you need to change the temperature.

This residential offer is available to Con Edison customers on a first-come, first-served basis while funding lasts. No contractor is required for this program—just install or register your device today to secure your reward and help support a more reliable energy grid for your community.
eligibility_summary: Receive an $85 rebate when you enroll an eligible smart thermostat in the Con Edison Smart Usage Rewards program. To qualify for this one-time payment, residential customers must register their device and agree to brief temperature adjustments during periods of high electricity demand. These events typically occur on weekdays for approximately four hours; however, you maintain full control and can override settings at any time. Incentives are available on a first-come, first-served basis while funding lasts. No contractor is required for this program.
application_process: Click Enroll Your Thermostat on the program page and complete enrollment. After enrolling, you’ll receive a confirmation email and an $85 rebate check within six to eight weeks.
notes: Limit 12 $85 rebates per household for enrollment. After enrolling, rebate check arrives within 6–8 weeks. Separate instant rebate available at Con Edison Marketplace with code SMARTREWARDS. Program enrollment processing may be delayed. Terms and conditions on manufacturers’ websites. First-come, first-served. A separate note on the page states limit one $100 rebate per household for equipment purchase (Marketplace offer).

program_name: Residential Renewable Energy Tax Credit
items: Solar Water Heat, Solar Photovoltaics, Biomass, Geothermal Heat Pumps, Wind (Small), Fuel Cells using Renewable Fuels
technologies: Solar Water Heat, Solar Photovoltaics, Geothermal Heat Pumps, Wind (Small), Fuel Cells using Renewable Fuels, Biomass
incentive_description: Note: Section 70506 of The One Big Beautiful Bill (OBBB) repealed this tax credit for any expenditures made after December 31, 2025. A taxpayer may claim a credit for a system that serves a dwelling unit located in the United States that is owned and used as a residence by the taxpayer. Expenditures with respect to the equipment are treated as made when the installation is completed. If the installation is at a new home, the "placed in service" date is the date of occupancy by the homeowner. Expenditures include labor costs for on-site preparation, assembly or original system installation, and for piping or wiring to interconnect a system to the home. If the federal tax credit exceeds tax liability, the excess amount may be carried forward to the succeeding taxable year. The maximum allowable credit, equipment requirements and other details vary by technology. Solar-electric property: 30% for systems placed in service by 12/31/2019; 26% for systems placed in service after 12/31/2019 and before 01/01/2022; 30% for systems placed in service after 12/31/2021 and expenditures made before 01/01/2026. Solar water-heating property: equipment must be SRCC-certified or comparable; at least half the energy used to heat the dwelling's water must be from solar; does not apply to pools or hot tubs. Fuel cell, small wind, geothermal, and standalone battery storage provisions and caps vary by technology.
special_requirements: Solar water heating property must be certified by SRCC or a comparable entity endorsed by the state where the system is installed. At least half the energy used to heat the dwelling's water must be from solar.
application_process: Consult tax instructions and claim the credit on your federal tax return (see IRS Form 5695). Review equipment requirements and eligibility before claiming. If credit exceeds tax liability, excess may be carried forward to the next tax year.
notes: The incentive is 30% of cost. Battery storage: 0% before 2023; 30% for systems placed in service after 12/31/2022 and expenditures made before 01/01/2026; standalone storage must have capacity of at least 3 kWh. Fuel cell maximum credit: $500 per 0.5 kW. Excess credit generally may be carried forward to next tax year. Last updated: 2025-07-25.

incentive_description: The Texas property tax code allows an exemption of the amount of 100% of the appraised property value increase arising from the installation or construction of a solar or wind-powered energy device that is primarily for the production and distribution of thermal, mechanical, or electrical energy for on-site use and devices used to store that energy. Under H.B. 2500 (2013), solar energy devices installed or constructed on or after January 1, 2014, used for a commercial purpose are subject to the cost method of appraisal, and the depreciated value must be calculated using a useful life of 10 years or less.
special_requirements: System must be used primarily for on-site energy needs; must meet definitions for solar or wind-powered energy devices as described in statute.
application_process: Those wishing to claim this exemption must fill out Form 50-123, “Exemption Application for Solar or Wind-Powered Energy Devices.” Visit the program website for guidelines and forms.
notes: 100% property value exemption. Eligible system size: None specified, but the system must be used primarily for on-site energy needs. Effective date: 2014-01-01. Authorities: Texas Tax Code § 11.27; Texas Tax Code § 23.26 (enacted 06/14/2013). Last updated: 2025-09-30.

program_name: Solar and Wind Energy Device Franchise Tax Deduction
items: Solar Water Heat, Solar Space Heat, Solar Thermal Electric, Solar Thermal Process Heat, Solar Photovoltaics, Wind (All)
technologies: Solar Water Heat, Solar Space Heat, Solar Thermal Electric, Solar Thermal Process Heat, Solar Photovoltaics, Wind (All)
incentive_description: Texas allows a corporation to deduct the cost of a solar energy device from the franchise tax in one of two ways: The total cost of the system may be deducted from the company's taxable capital or, 10% of the system's cost may be deducted from the company's income. Both taxable capital and a company's income are taxed under the franchise tax, which is Texas's equivalent to a corporate tax. For the purposes of this deduction, a solar energy device means "a system or series of mechanisms designed primarily to provide heating or cooling or to produce electrical or mechanical power by collecting and transferring solar-generated energy." Under this definition wind energy is also included as an eligible technology. Texas also offers a franchise tax exemption for manufacturers, seller, or installers of solar energy systems which also includes wind energy as an eligible technology.
special_requirements: Must meet definitions for a solar energy device; wind energy also included as eligible technology.
application_process: Contact the program administrator or visit the program website to apply. Review eligibility requirements before starting your project.
notes: Incentive amount: 10% of amortized cost (option to deduct total cost from taxable capital or 10% from income). Texas also offers a franchise tax exemption for manufacturers, sellers, or installers of solar energy systems. Last updated: 2025-05-21.

incentive_description: NOTE: The most recent solicitation for this program closed February 28, 2025. Please check the program website for information on future solicitations. The U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) offers an ongoing grant program for the improvement of energy generation, transmission, and distribution facilities in rural communities. This program began in 2000. Eligibility is limited to projects in communities that have average home energy costs at least 275% above the national average. Retail power suppliers serving rural areas are eligible to apply for grant funding, including non-profits (cooperatives and limited dividend or mutual associations), commercial entities, state and local governments entities, and tribal governments. Under the most recent solicitation for projects, a total of $7 million was available for qualifying projects. Under this solicitation grants ranging from $100,000 to $3 million were available for a variety of activities, including: Electric generation, transmission, and distribution facilities; Natural gas or petroleum storage or distribution facilities; Renewable energy facilities used for on-grid or off-grid electric power generation, water or space heating, or process heating and power; Backup up or emergency power generation or energy storage equipment; and Weatherization of residential and community property, or other energy efficiency or conservation programs. This grant program is not limited to renewable energy or energy conservation and efficiency measures, but these measures are eligible for this grant program.

special_requirements: Both grid-connected and off-grid renewable energy installations are eligible

technologies: Water Heaters, Furnaces, Boilers, Heat pumps, Air conditioners, Duct/Air sealing, Building Insulation, Windows, Doors, Roofs, Heat Pump Water Heater, Electrical Panel, Smart Electrical Panel, Biomass Stoves
incentive_description: Note: Section 70505 of The One Big Beautiful Bill (OBBB) repealed this tax credit for equipment placed in service after December 31, 2025. Property placed in service after 12/31/2022 and before 1/1/2026: H.R. 5376 of 2022 modified the value of this tax credit, making it 30% of the cost of an item or improvement. Each item or improvement has a separate tax credit cap as shown below, and a taxpayer cannot claim more than $1,200 in total tax credits per year, except as specified. Building Envelope Improvements: Owners of existing homes may receive a tax credit worth 30% of the cost of upgrading the efficiency of the building's envelope. Installation (labor) costs are not included and the improvement must meet the specified efficiency standards. Qualified Energy Property: Taxpayers who purchase qualified residential energy-efficient property may be eligible for a tax credit. The equipment must meet or exceed the highest efficiency tier established by the Consortium for Energy Efficiency in effect as of the beginning of the calendar year in which the equipment is placed in service. The credit is equal to 30% of the cost of the equipment. Certain technologies have higher caps (heat pumps, heat pump water heaters, biomass stoves).
special_requirements: Equipment must be new and in compliance with all applicable performance and safety standards as described in tax code
application_process: No application with the program administrator is required; claim the credit when filing federal income taxes using IRS guidance and Form 5695. Review eligibility requirements, equipment specifications, and applicable caps before claiming.
notes: For purchases made in 2023 - 2025: annual maximum of $1,200, with exceptions for certain technologies (e.g., heat pumps, heat pump water heaters, biomass stoves). For purchases made in 2005 - 2022: aggregate cap of $500 (with historical variations including $1,500 for 2009-2010). Specific caps: heat pump water heater $2,000; heat pump $2,000; biomass stove $2,000; central AC $600; many other items $600 or lower. Last updated: 2025-07-25.

items: Solar Photovoltaics, Low-Flow Water Fixtures, Clothes Washers, Dishwasher, Dehumidifiers, Water Heaters, Lighting, Furnaces, Boilers, Heat pumps, Air conditioners, Caulking/Weather-stripping, Duct/Air sealing, Building Insulation, Windows, Roofs, Comprehensive Measures/Whole Building, Custom/Others pending approval, Insulation, Tankless Water Heater

incentive_description: NOTE: Only multifamily properties are eligible for the program. Single family homeowners are not eligible for this program. The Fannie Mae Green Financing Business provides mortgage financing to apartment buildings and cooperatives (with 5 or more units) to finance energy and water efficiency property improvements. Its green financing programs include Green Rewards, and preferential pricing for loans secured by a property with an eligible Green Building Certification. All Fannie Mae green loans are securitized as Green Mortgage Backed Securities (Green MBS). Green Rewards provides preferential pricing and up to an additional 5% of loan proceeds by including projected owner and tenant energy and water savings in underwriting. To qualify for a Green Rewards loan the property owner must commit to making property improvements projected to reduce whole property's annual energy and water consumption by at least 30% (minimum 15% energy). Properties may be located anywhere in US, and selected property upgrades must be completed within 12 months of loan closing.
special_requirements: Only multifamily properties (apartment buildings and cooperatives with 5 or more units) are eligible. For Green Rewards, property improvements must be projected to reduce whole property's annual energy and water consumption by at least 30% (minimum 15% energy). Selected upgrades must be completed within 12 months of loan closing. Must coordinate with a Fannie Mae DUS Lender.
application_process: Contact a Fannie Mae DUS® Lender or visit the program website to inquire about loan products (Green Rewards, preferential pricing) and application steps. Review eligibility requirements for multifamily properties (5+ units) and required green improvements before applying.
notes: Interest rate benefit varies – contact a Fannie Mae DUS® Lender for details. Maximum Loan: Not Specified. Loan Term: Not Specified. Last updated: 2025-05-16.

program_name: Save $2,000 to $10,000 on an HVAC Air-Source Heat Pump
items: Keep old heating system and install integrated controls (Enhanced Incentives)
incentive_description:
Get between $2,000 and $10,000 back when you upgrade your home heating and cooling system through Con Edison. This program helps New York residents cover a significant portion of their project costs, with incentives capped at 70% of the total price. If your home is located in a designated Disadvantaged Community, you may be eligible to have up to 85% of your project costs covered.

To claim these savings, you must install an energy-efficient air-source heat pump with integrated controls while keeping your existing heating system as a backup. This setup allows you to maximize your comfort and efficiency year-round. All work must be completed by a qualified contractor to ensure your system is installed correctly and qualifies for the rebate.

This opportunity is available to residents in single-family homes, two-to-four-family buildings, and individual apartment owners in larger buildings. Whether you are looking to lower your energy bills or improve your home's climate control, these rebates make the transition to modern heat pump technology more affordable than ever.
bonus_condition: Up to 85% of project costs if located in a Disadvantaged Community (vs. 70% standard cap).
special_requirements: Contractor's receipt and final inspection.
building_type: Single Family Homes and Multi-Family Building with 4 Units or Less
eligibility_summary: Single-family homes, two- to four-family buildings, and individual apartment owners in buildings with five or more apartments.
application_process: Choose from Con Edison’s approved contractors (or have yours apply to participate). The contractor will scope the project and provide pricing after Con Edison’s contribution. Contractor performs the work and subtracts incentives directly from your invoice. After installation, enroll in Con Edison’s Select Pricing Plan.
notes: Program page emphasizes using participating contractors; incentives are deducted on the contractor invoice. Page footer shows © 2026 Con Edison; no specific end date found.

program_name: Energy-Efficient Mortgages
items: Solar - Passive, Solar Water Heat, Solar Space Heat, Solar Photovoltaics, Daylighting
technologies: Solar - Passive, Solar Water Heat, Solar Space Heat, Solar Photovoltaics, Daylighting
incentive_description: Homeowners can take advantage of energy efficient mortgages (EEM) to either finance energy efficiency improvements to existing homes, including renewable energy technologies, or to increase their home buying power with the purchase of a new energy efficient home. The U.S. federal government supports these loans by insuring them through Federal Housing Authority (FHA) or Veterans Affairs (VA) programs. This allows borrowers who might otherwise be denied loans to pursue energy efficiency, and it secures lenders against loan default. The FHA allows lenders to add up to 100% of energy efficiency improvements to an existing mortgage loan with certain restrictions. The maximum amount of the portion of an energy efficient mortgage allowed for energy improvements is the lesser of 5% of: the value of the property; 115% of the median area price of a single-family dwelling; 150% of the Freddie Mac conforming loan limit. Borrowers must obtain a home energy assessment by a qualified energy rater, assessor, or auditor. VA EEMs allow borrowers to add up to $3,000 (with documentation) or up to $6,000 if projected savings exceed payment increases; loans may exceed this amount at VA discretion. Conventional lenders (Fannie Mae/Freddie Mac) may allow up to 15% of a home's appraised value for improvements.
special_requirements: Borrowers must obtain a home energy assessment (HER) by a qualified rater, assessor, or auditor. VA applicants must submit contractor bids, HER and certificate of eligibility where applicable. Must meet program-specific documentation requirements.
application_process: Contact an FHA-, VA-, Fannie Mae- or Freddie Mac-approved lender or local HUD/VA field office to apply. Review eligibility requirements and provide required documentation (home energy assessment, contractor bids, certificate of eligibility for VA where applicable).
notes: Maximum loan limits can be exceeded by the energy improvements being financed. Last updated: 2025-05-16.

incentive_description: The U.S. Department of Energy's (DOE) Office of Indian Energy Policy and Programs promotes tribal energy sufficiency, economic growth, and employment on tribal lands through the development of renewable energy and energy efficiency technologies. The program provides financial assistance, technical assistance, and education and training to tribes for the evaluation and development of renewable energy resources and energy efficiency measures. DOE's program offerings consist of program management through DOE headquarters, program implementation and project management through DOE's field offices, and technical support through DOE laboratories. Program management is carried out by DOE's Weatherization and Intergovernmental Program, which provides programmatic direction and funding to DOE field offices for program implementation. incentive_description: Purchases of certain energy-efficient products during Memorial Day weekend are exempt from the state sales and use tax. This 3-day tax holiday begins on the Saturday before the last Monday in May (Memorial Day) and ends on that same Monday (Memorial Day). The state sales and use tax rate is 6.25%; some local jurisdictions add additional local sales and use taxes of up to 2%. The state exemption applies to both the state and local portion (if one exists) of the tax. Although the eligibility of some products is limited according to their sale price, there are no limitations on the total value or number of products exempt from sales tax.
special_requirements: Must meet federal ENERGY STAR efficiency requirements
application_process: No application needed. Purchasers are not required to supply an exemption certificate or any other form of documentation in order to claim the exemption. See program website for details on internet/catalog sales, delivery charges, layaway plans, and installation charges.

program_name: Energy-Efficient New Homes Tax Credit for Home Builders
items: Comprehensive Measures/Whole Building
technologies: Comprehensive Measures/Whole Building
incentive_description: Note: Section 70508 of The One Big Beautiful Bill (OBBB) repealed this tax credit for homes constructed and acquired after June 30, 2026. Tax Credit for homes constructed and acquired before January 1, 2023: The federal Energy Policy Act of 2005 established tax credits of up to $2,000 for builders of all new energy-efficient homes, including manufactured homes constructed in accordance with the Federal Manufactured Homes Construction and Safety Standards. The home qualifies for the credit if: It is located in the United States; Its construction is substantially completed before December 31, 2021; It meets the energy saving requirements outlined in the statute; and It is acquired from the eligible contractor after December 31, 2013, and before January 1, 2022, for use as a residence. Energy Saving Requirements: Site-built homes qualify for a $2,000 credit if they are certified to reduce heating and cooling energy consumption by 50% relative to the IECC 2006 and meet DOE minimum efficiency standards. Manufactured homes may qualify for $2,000 or $1,000 depending on standards met, or $1,000 if ENERGY STAR. Certification: IRS guidance details the certification process and approved software. Tax Credit for homes constructed and acquired after December 31, 2022 and before January 1, 2026: The IRA of 2022 established new criteria and values. Single-family and manufactured homes meeting Energy Star requirements can receive $2,500; zero energy ready homes can receive $5,000. Multifamily credits vary by unit ($500, $2,500 with prevailing wages) and zero energy ready ($1,000, $5,000 with prevailing wages).
application_process: Contact the program administrator or visit the program website to apply. Review eligibility requirements before starting your project.
notes: Varies, see summary below. Maximum Incentive: $5,000. Last updated: 2025-07-25.
technical_details: Certification guidance: IRS Notice 2006-27 and IRS Notice 2006-28; IRS Form 8908. IRS Notice 2023-65 provides guidance for credits under the Inflation Reduction Act.

items: Solar Water Heat, Solar Space Heat, Geothermal Electric, Solar Thermal Electric, Solar Photovoltaics, Wind, Biomass, Hydroelectric, Hydrogen, Geothermal Heat Pumps, Combined Heat & Power, Tidal, Wave, Ocean Thermal, Wind (Small), Hydroelectric (Small), Geothermal Direct-Use, Anaerobic Digestion, Fuel Cells using Renewable Fuels, Microturbines

incentive_description: The Rural Energy for America Program (REAP) provides financial assistance to agricultural producers and rural small businesses to purchase, install, and construct renewable energy systems, make energy efficiency improvements to non‑residential buildings and facilities, use renewable technologies that reduce energy consumption, and participate in energy audits and renewable energy development assistance. Grants are limited to 25% of a project's cost; a loan guarantee may not exceed $25,000,000. The combined amount of a grant and loan guarantee must be at least $5,000 (with the grant portion at least $1,500) and may not exceed 75% of the project’s cost. A minimum of 20% of funds is generally dedicated to grants of $20,000 or less. Application due dates are published annually in the Notice of Funding Availability.
incentive_narrative: Grants and guaranteed loans available to agricultural producers and rural small businesses; specific technologies and project eligibility described in program materials.
special_requirements: Applicants must demonstrate sufficient revenue to cover operations, maintenance, and applicable debt service for the duration of the guaranteed loan or grant. Rural small businesses must be located in rural areas; agricultural producers may be located in non‑rural areas. Working capital and land acquisition eligible only for loan guarantees.
application_process: Contact the program administrator or visit the program website to apply. Review eligibility requirements and Notice of Funding Availability for application due dates.
notes: Loan guarantees on loans up to 75% of total eligible project costs. Grants limited to 25% of project cost; combined grant and loan guarantee minimum $5,000 and grant portion at least $1,500. Loan guarantee cap referenced at $25,000,000. Rates and terms negotiated with lender and subject to USDA approval. Last updated: 2024-10-09.
technical_details: Eligible project costs include purchasing energy efficiency improvements or a renewable energy system, energy audits or assessments, permitting and licensing fees, business plans and retrofitting. For new construction, replacement of older equipment may be eligible only when a new facility is planned to be more efficient and similarly sized than the older facility.

program_name: Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
funding_source: U.S. Dept. of Health and Human Services
administrator: U.S. Dept. of Health and Human Services
items: Home energy bills, Energy crises, Weatherization, Energy-related minor home repairs
technologies: Weatherization, Energy-related minor home repairs
incentive_description: The Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP) provides resources to assist families with energy costs. This federally funded assistance helps in managing costs associated with home energy bills, energy crises, and weatherization and energy-related minor home repairs. The program is available in all 50 U.S. states, Indian Tribe or Tribal organization and U.S. territories that include: American Samoa, Guam, the Northern Mariana Islands, Puerto Rico and the Virgin Islands. LIHEAP income eligibility uses 150 percent of the poverty level (or 60 percent of state median income if higher) and may not be set lower than 110 percent of the poverty level. Applicants will need to provide documentation such as utility bills, proof of income, proof of address, household member documentation, Social Security numbers, and proof of U.S. citizenship or permanent residence. The availability of LIHEAP assistance is not guaranteed; federal funds are often spent during the winter.
special_requirements: The LIHEAP program may require households to meet additional eligibility criteria to receive LIHEAP assistance.
application_process: Contact the program administrator or visit the program website to apply. Review eligibility requirements before starting your application. Potential applicants can call the National Energy Assistance Referral (NEAR) project or contact their state/territory/tribal LIHEAP office for application details.
notes: Incentive Amount: Varies. Maximum Incentive: Varies. National program available in all 50 states, territories, and tribal organizations. Applicants must provide documentation such as utility bills, proof of income, proof of address, household member documentation, Social Security numbers, and proof of U.S. citizenship or permanent residence. Last updated: 2022-11-18.

incentive_description: Note: The One Big Beautiful Bill (OBBB) made significant changes to this tax credit. To qualify for the tax credit, solar and wind energy systems must be either placed in service by December 31, 2027, or construction must commence by July 4, 2026. An Executive Order issued on July 7, 2025 directed the Secretary of Treasury to issue new and revised guidance within 45 days to ensure that policies concerning the 'beginning of construction' are not circumvented. Additionally, the tax credit is not available for systems that commenced construction after December 31, 2025 if the facility or property includes any material assistance from a prohibited foreign entity. The tax credit for non-solar and wind technologies (combined heat and power, energy storage, hydropower, and geothermal) will experience step-downs depending on construction commencement year. Base credit and various bonus credits (Domestic Content, Energy Community, Low-Income, etc.) and monetization/transfer provisions apply as described by law and Treasury/IRS guidance.
special_requirements: Projects over 1 MW may need to meet prevailing wage and apprenticeship requirements to qualify for higher credit rates; certain domestic content, energy community, and low-income location requirements apply. Some technologies have construction-date or price limitations as noted in program guidance.
customer_type: Commercial, Industrial, Investor-Owned Utility, Municipal Utilities, Cooperative Utilities, Agricultural

notes: Base Credit: 6% - 30% (depending on project status and labor factors). Domestic Content Bonus: 10% additional. Energy Community Bonus: 10% additional. Low-Income Community Bonus: 10% additional. Low-income Residential Building or Low-Income Economic Benefit Bonus: 20% additional. Credit carries over indefinitely. Last updated: 2025-07-16.

items: Solar Water Heat, Solar Space Heat, Solar Thermal Electric, Solar Thermal Process Heat, Solar Photovoltaics, Wind (All)

incentive_description: Companies in Texas engaged solely in the business of manufacturing, selling, or installing solar or wind energy devices are exempt from the franchise tax. The franchise tax is Texas’s equivalent to a corporate tax. There is no ceiling on this exemption, so it can be a substantial incentive for solar and wind businesses. For the purposes of this exemption, a solar energy device means "a system or series of mechanisms designed primarily to provide heating or cooling or to produce electrical or mechanical power by collecting and transferring solar-generated energy. The term includes a mechanical or chemical device that has the ability to store solar-generated energy for use in heating or cooling or in the production of power." Under this definition wind energy is also listed as an eligible technology. Texas also offers a franchise tax deduction for solar energy devices which also includes wind energy as an eligible technology.
special_requirements: Must meet definition for solar or wind energy devices as specified by statute (devices designed primarily to provide heating or cooling or to produce electrical or mechanical power; includes storage devices).
application_process: No application process required through DSIRE; businesses claiming the exemption should follow Comptroller of Public Accounts guidance and relevant tax filing procedures. Contact the administrator or visit the program website for statutory guidance.
notes: Incentive: 100% exemption. Maximum Incentive: All (no ceiling). Terms: N/A. Last updated: 2025-05-21.
